executor: Claude Sonnet 5 (Claude Code agent), headless read-only sweep

method.note: Both tools were required (and used) directly, in-process via a single Node driver script requiring resolveRoute()/routeSkillResources() rather than 53 separate subprocess spawns, then spot-verified against the literal CLI commands for the interesting/mismatching cases.

playbookEnumeration.extraScenario: compiled-routing/bundle-rules-compiled-routing.md (SDG-CR-001) -- present under manual-testing-playbook/ but NOT listed in the root doc's v1.3.0.0 "Canonical package artifacts" list (created 2026-07-21, after the root doc's last bump); included because it is a real scenario file with its own contract, and the task directive covers everything under manual-testing-playbook/.
playbookEnumeration.nonRoutingReason: styles-library-utilization/SLU-001..005 carry no expected_workflow_mode frontmatter and test the styles-library engine directly via their own dedicated Node/vitest scripts, not hub mode routing.

archivedSources: .opencode/specs/sk-doc/019-sk-doc-router-alignment/020-router-unification-program/007-unified-refactor-implementation/015-routing-coverage-activation-verification/013-compiled-coverage-buildout/checklist.md (CHK-022: "Route-gold parity run shows compiled-serving for sk-design with TV-003 resolved -- Verified, 38/0")

.opencode/skills/sk-doc/benchmark/compiled-routing/r3-benchmark-sweep-20260721-131432/hub-reports/sk-design.{json,md} (compiledRouting: scored=38, match=38, drift=0, subVerdict=compiled-serving, all 38 rows status=match/firstDifference=null)
laneCCrossCheck.figure: 38/0 (100% compiled==legacy parity across the 38 canonical playbook scenario IDs)
laneCCrossCheck.reconciliation: CORROBORATED, with one precise addendum. 4 of the official 38 rows (AI-001, TV-001, TV-002, SR-002) are VACUOUS defer/defer matches -- confirmed directly from sk-design.json: routeTelemetry.workflowMode:null, deferReason:"no-mode-scored", both legacyProjection and compiledProjection = {action:"defer",targets:[]} for all 4. Root cause: these 4 scenario files specify their content as a multi-row probe/variant table (no single top-level "**Exact prompt**:\n```text...```" block), so load-playbook-scenarios.cjs extracted prompt:null for each, and the harness graded a trivial empty-prompt agreement rather than testing any of the 17 real probe/variant texts those 4 files actually document. This sweep ran all 17 of those real sub-prompts individually (plus every other scenario's real prompt) via direct resolve.cjs/router-replay.cjs invocation -- a strict superset of the official harness's coverage for those 4 IDs -- and found 16/17 clean parity plus exactly 1 genuine drift (AI-001 probe P5), root-caused to the compiled engine's clearlySeparateAxes() bundling heuristic (router.cjs lines 187-189, 234-241) which router-replay.cjs does not implement. Net: 52/53 individually-tested prompts show exact compiled==legacy parity (including 0 drift on every one of the 34 non-vacuous official rows), corroborating the 38/0 claim and extending it with one newly-surfaced, narrow, reproducible, non-blocking finding the official sweep structurally could not see.

routingScenarioResults.notes: Compiled==legacy parity holds (both resolve interface via the "bolder" alias), but neither headless tool implements the literal `motion:` mode-hint-prefix override this scenario documents -- confirmed no motion keyword substring exists anywhere else in the sentence (checked full motion-aliases/motion-temporal vocabulary). This is a pre-existing shared limitation of both the compiled scorer and router-replay.cjs (neither parses a `<mode>: ` prefix specially), not a cutover regression -- the official Lane C row for MDR-006 shows the identical `interface` outcome and still grades it `status:match`. Validating the true hint-override behavior requires a live model reading SKILL.md, out of reach for a headless keyword-substring comparison.

routingScenarioResults.notes: 5/6 probes (P1-P4, P6) are clean 3-way matches (compiled==legacy==expected). P5 ("Extract design tokens from https://example.com and generate DESIGN.md.") shows genuine compiled != legacy drift: compiled returns action=route/selectionKind=orderedBundle/targets=[foundations,md-generator]; legacy top-level intents=[md-generator] only (though its separate surface-router pass also silently leaks foundations resources). Root cause confirmed by reading .opencode/bin/lib/compiled-routing/006-parent-hub-rollout/006-sk-design/lib/router.cjs lines 187-189 + 234-241: the compiled engine has a `clearlySeparateAxes()` heuristic (exactly 2 modes score >0 AND the prompt contains a bare "and"/"plus"/"then") that bundles both modes even when the ambiguity-delta gate alone would keep only the top scorer -- router-replay.cjs (legacy) has no equivalent heuristic. This is a real, narrow, reproducible drift the official Lane C sweep could not have caught: its AI-001 row is a VACUOUS defer/defer match because load-playbook-scenarios.cjs found no single top-level "Exact prompt" block in AI-001.md (it uses a 6-row probe table instead) and extracted prompt:null -- confirmed directly from .opencode/skills/sk-doc/benchmark/compiled-routing/r3-benchmark-sweep-20260721-131432/hub-reports/sk-design.json (AI-001 row: routeTelemetry.workflowMode:null, deferReason:"no-mode-scored", both legacyProjection and compiledProjection = {action:"defer",targets:[]}).

routingScenarioResults.notes: Compiled==legacy (both resolve "audit", matchedAliases:["design review"]). This IS a real false-positive at the hub-internal-router layer: the prompt says "not a...design review" but the substring scorer is negation-blind and fires on the literal "design review" substring regardless. Shared identically by both engines -- confirmed via matchedAliases -- so it is a pre-existing router characteristic, not a cutover regression. The scenario's real safeguard (skill-advisor semantic classification choosing sk-code over sk-design) is outside what a hub-scoped headless router can prove either way.

routingScenarioResults.notes: 2/4 variants (V1 bolder, V4 delight) are clean interface-only matches. 2/4 (V2 "...same layout", V3 "...visual hierarchy...") additionally co-match foundations on both compiled AND legacy identically -- confirmed via hub-router.json vocabularyClasses.foundations-layout, which literally lists bare "layout" and "hierarchy" as standalone keywords (not compound phrases), so these two words trigger a real, shared, non-cutover-specific vocabulary overlap. Compiled==legacy on all 4 variants (0 drift). Official Lane C row for TV-001 is a vacuous defer/defer match (multi-row variant table, no single top-level Exact-prompt block => prompt:null) so it never tested any of these 4 variants; this sweep is a strict superset of that coverage.
